questions.question: In Bresenham's line algorithm, what does the decision parameter (error term) represent?
options: The Euclidean distance from the current pixel to the ideal line
Twice the signed vertical distance from the midpoint between two candidate pixels to the true line position
The slope of the line multiplied by the current x coordinate
The accumulated rounding error in floating-point y coordinates
questions.explanation: The decision parameter is 2·dy·(x - x0) - 2·dx·(y - y0) + dx, which equals twice the signed distance from the midpoint between (x+1, y) and (x+1, y+1) to the true line. This eliminates floating-point arithmetic entirely — the sign of this integer determines which pixel to pick.

questions.question: Why does Bresenham's algorithm avoid all floating-point operations?
options: It approximates the line slope with a lookup table
It uses fixed-point arithmetic with implicit scaling
The decision parameter and its increments are derived from integer-only expressions: 2·dy and 2·(dy - dx), so only integer additions and sign checks are needed
It rounds floating-point values to the nearest integer at each step
questions.explanation: Starting from an integer initial value d = 2·dy - dx, the update rules are d += 2·dy (step right) or d += 2·(dy - dx) (step diagonally). All values and increments are integers by construction, so no floats ever appear.

questions.question: Given triangle vertices V0, V1, V2 in counter-clockwise order, a point P has edge function values E01(P) = 3, E12(P) = 5, E20(P) = 2. What can you conclude?
options: P is outside the triangle because the values are not equal
P is inside the triangle because all three edge function values are positive
P is on the boundary because one value is close to zero
P is inside the triangle because the values sum to 10, which equals the triangle area
questions.explanation: For counter-clockwise winding, a point is inside the triangle if and only if all three edge functions evaluate to a non-negative value. Since 3, 5, 2 are all positive, P is strictly inside (not on any edge).

questions.question: Barycentric coordinates (w0, w1, w2) are computed as ratios of sub-triangle areas to the total triangle area. What property makes them useful for interpolation?
options: They are always integers, so interpolation is exact
They sum to 1.0, so any vertex attribute A at point P can be interpolated as A(P) = w0·A0 + w1·A1 + w2·A2
They are invariant under perspective projection, so no correction is ever needed
They are unique to each triangle shape, so they can be used as a hash key
questions.explanation: Because w0 + w1 + w2 = 1 (the three sub-areas partition the full triangle), any quantity stored at the vertices — color, depth, texture coordinates, normals — can be linearly interpolated using these weights. This is the foundation of all per-fragment attribute interpolation in GPUs.

questions.question: What problem does the top-left fill rule solve?
options: It determines which triangle is drawn on top when two triangles overlap in depth
It ensures triangles with horizontal edges are always rasterized before triangles with vertical edges
It resolves the ambiguity when a pixel center lies exactly on a shared edge, preventing both double-rendering and gaps between adjacent triangles
It defines the winding order convention so that clockwise and counter-clockwise triangles are rasterized identically
questions.explanation: When a pixel center falls exactly on a shared edge, two adjacent triangles might both claim it (double-write) or neither might (gap). The top-left rule assigns the pixel to the triangle for which the shared edge is classified as a 'top' edge (horizontal, interior below) or 'left' edge (the triangle's interior is to the left). This guarantees exactly one owner per pixel on shared edges.

questions.question: Why are triangles — and not quads or n-gons — the universal rasterization primitive?
options: Triangles are faster to draw because they have fewer vertices
Any polygon can be triangulated; triangles are always planar and convex with an unambiguous interior; n-gons can be self-intersecting, non-planar, or concave
GPU hardware only has three vertex attribute slots per primitive
Triangles require fewer edge function evaluations than quads, making them more efficient per pixel
questions.explanation: Three points always define a plane (no non-planar ambiguity). Triangles are always convex (every line between two interior points stays inside). Their interior is unambiguous (no winding or self-intersection issues). Any polygon can be decomposed into triangles, so supporting only triangles is sufficient. Quads can be non-planar; n-gons can be self-intersecting.
